Wilde, WJJO de. 1979. New account of the genus Knema (Myristicaceae). Blumea 25: 321-478
Wilde, WJJO de. 1981. Supplementary data on Malesian Knema (Myristicaceae) including three new taxa. Blumea 27: 223-234
Wilde, WJJO de. 1984. Endocomia, a new genus of Myristicaceae. Blumea 30: 173-196

Wilde, WJJO de. 1985. A new account of the genus Horsfieldia (Myristicaceae), part 1. Gardens’ Bulletin Singapore 37: 115-179; part. 2. ibid. 38 (1985) 55-144; part 3. ibid. 38 (1985) 185-226; part. 4. ibid. 39 (1985) 1-65.
Wilde, WJJO de. 1987. Further supplementary data on Malesian Knema (Myristicaceae). Blumea 32: 115-141
Wilde, WJJO de. 1996. Additional notes on species of the Asian genera Endocomia, Horsfieldia, and Knema (Myristicaceae). Blumea 41: 375-396

Wilde, WJJO de. 1998. More notes on Knema and Myristica (Myristicaceae). Blumea 43: 241-254
Wilde, WJJO de. 2000. Myristicaceae. Flora Malesiana ser. 1, 14: 1-632

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 11 November 2017

species.citation: (Miq.) W.J.de Wilde , Blumea 30 (1984) 182 ; Blumea 41 (1996) 375; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 14 (2000) 32; ≡ Myristica macrocoma Miq. , Ann. Mus. Bot. Lugduno-Batavi 1 (1864) 207. ssp. macrocoma. Distribution: Moluccas, Philippines, ?Sulawesi. LUZON. Notes: According to De Wilde (2000), the Palanan material strongly deviates in its very hairy appearance, and may represent a separate taxon. Forest with little undergrowth, on alluvial flats locally with stagnant water, or hill slopes; on porous volcanic soil, or loam soil with stones; also in disturbed forest. Fl. & fr. throughout the year. 0-400m. Native. ssp. prainii (King) W.J.de Wilde, Blumea 30 (1984) 187; Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 14 (2000) 35; ≡ Myristica prainii King, Ann. Roy. Bot. Gard. (Calcutta) 3 (1891) 299. = Horsfieldia oblongata Merr., PJS 13c (1918) 286; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 182. = Horsfieldia merrillii Warb., Fragm. Fl. Philipp. 1 (1904) 49. Distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, China, India, Indochina, Java, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra. BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, LEYTE, LUZON: Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Quezon, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR, TABLAS. Notes: Lowland and hillside forest, riverine or swamp forest; by streams, on alluvial or clayey soil, in limestone country, or on copper-rich soil. Fl. & fr. throughout the year, but in Philippines mainly Mar-May. 0-500m. Native.
species.distribution: BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, CEBU (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Quezon, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR, TABLAS.

species.citation: (Wall. ex Hook.f. & Thomson) Warb. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 68 (1897) 365 ; R.T.A.Schouten, Blumea 31 (1986) 476; De Wilde, Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 14 (2000) 44; ≡ Myristica farquhariana Wall. ex Hook.f. & Thomson , Fl. Ind. (Hooker f. & Thomson) 1 (1855) 161. var. paniculata (A.DC.) R.T.A.Schouten, Blumea 31 (1986) 481; De Wilde, Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 14 (2000) 47; ≡ Gymnacranthera paniculata (A.DC.) Warb., Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 68 (1897) 370; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 181; ≡ Myristica paniculata A.DC., Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 4th Sér. 4 (1855) 31. = Gymnacranthera acuminata Merr., PJS 12c (1917) 265; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 181. = Gymnacranthera macrobotrys Merr., PJS 13c (1918) 284; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 181. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, CALAYAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TABLAS, TICAO. Notes: Forests, on ridges, as well as by rivers and lakes, common. 0-1400m. Native. var. zippeliana (Miq.) R.T.A.Schouten, Blumea 31 (1986) 482; De Wilde, Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 14 (2000) 47; ≡ Myristica zippeliana Miq., Ann. Mus. Bot. Lugduno-Batavi 2 (1865) 50. = Gymnacranthera suluensis Warb., Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 68 (1897) 373; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 181; Type: Vidal 3546 (syn); Vidal 3561 (syn), Basilan. Distribution: Bismarck Arch, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines. BASILAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO. Notes: 0-1400m. Native.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BASILAN, BUCAS GRANDE (photos), CALAYAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet (photos), Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TABLAS, TAWI-TAWI, TICAO.

species.notes: Mixed rain forest, primary dipterocarp forest; recorded from alluvial soil and volcanic soil, with Eucalyptus deglupta dominance. 50-1200m.

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2775 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 178; J.Sinclair, Gard. Bull. Sing. 23 (1968) 218; De Wilde, Blumea 42 (1997) 146; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 14 (2000) 398; Type: Elmer 13284 (L, iso), Mindanao: Agusan del Norte, Mt Urdaneta, Jul-1912. = Myristica laevis ssp. badia W.J.de Wilde , Blumea 42 (1997) 172 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 14 (2000) 509; Type: Rojo 159 (L, holo), Mindanao: Surigao del Sur, Lanuza. Hillside forest, 400m, Oct/Nov-1972. ssp. agusanensis. = Myristica sorsogonensis Elmer ex Merr., EPFP 2 (1923) 178, nom. inval., in obs., pro syn.; Elmer, LPB 10 (1939) 3809, nom. inval., no Latin descr.; Voucher: Elmer 16010 (L, NY), Luzon: Sorsogon, Mt Bulusan, May-1916. = Myristica laxiflora Merr., PJS 17 (1920) 254; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 179; Type: BS 15498 Reillo (L, NY, US, iso), Basilan. Aug-1912. Throughout the Philippines, except Palawan. Inland forests at low elevation (50 m). Fl. Jul-Aug.; fr. throughout the year. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, LUZON: Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Sur, NEGROS, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO. Notes: 50m. Native. ssp. squamulosa W.J.de Wilde, Blumea 42 (1997) 146; ≡ Gymnacranthera lanceolata Merr., PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 55; Elmer, LPB 3 (1911) 1058; ≡ Myristica lancifolia Merr., EPFP 2 (1923) 178, nom. illeg., non Poir.; Type: FB 3236 Meyer, Luzon. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Cagayan, Camarines Sur, SIBUYAN. Native.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, LUZON: Bataan, Cagayan, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Surigao del Sur, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO.
species.notes: If Myristica agusanensis Elmer and Gymnacranthera lanceolata Merr. are considered conspecific, the epithet of the latter name is the oldest available for this species (and is not blocked by Myristica lanceolata Wall. 1828, a nomen nudum with no nomenclatural priority). The combination Myristica lanceolata (Merr.) ined. therefore needs to be made to replace Myristica agusanensis Elmer as the correct name, because the epithet of Gymnacranthera lanceolata Merr. (1906) has priority over the epithet of Myristica agusanensis Elmer (1915) (John Boggan, pers. comm. 3-Nov-18). 50-1380m.

species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Isabela.
species.notes: Forest on ultramafics, low stature forest with many large-girth trees, streamside forests, or high canopy forest on low coastal hills; locally common on coastal flat, riverine areas. Fl & fr. Apr. 0-50m.

species.citation: Warb. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 68 (1897) 442 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 178; De Wilde, Blumea 42 (1997) 154; Fl. species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. species.notes: Forests, common. Fl. mainly May-Aug; fr. throughout the year. 0-1000m.

species.notes: Philippine record, fide De Wilde (2000), is based on Wenzel 3537 (K) which agrees with specimens from NE Borneo. Its locality however is remarkable, far from main distributional area of the species. 0-1200(-1800)m.

species.citation: W.J.de Wilde , Blumea 42 (1997) 180 ; Fl. Males. Ser. 1, 14 (2000) 554; Type: Ridsdale SMHI 258 (L, holo), Palawan: Palawan, Puerto Princesa, Mt Beaufort, W spur, 815m, 29-Mar-1984. var. rubrinervis. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN. Notes: Known from Puerto Princesa: Mt Beaufort. Stunted montane forest with many epiphytes but little moss; closed broad-leaved rainforest on ultramafics. Fr. Mar-May. 400-900m. Native. var. duplex W.J.de Wilde, Blumea 42 (1997) 182; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 14 (2000) 555; Type: Ridsdale SMHI 1528 (L, holo), Palawan: Pagdanan Range, Ibangley, Brookside Hill. Closed broad-leaved rainforest with Dipterocarpus spp., 40m, 23-Apr-1984. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE, MINDORO, PALAWAN. Notes: Lowland dipterocarp to montane ridge forests (with Agathis). Fl. (immature) & fr. Feb-Apr. 40-900m. Native.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE, MINDANAO: Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN.
